briefing.headline: Rig up a Neko Rig — aggressive winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Neko Rig is a strong choice for winter with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Use a Neko rig around docks when primary offshore spots get pressured — dock fish are often less beat up and can save a tournament late in the day

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a Neko rig around docks when primary offshore spots get pressured — dock fish are often less beat up and can save a tournament late in the day
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on rock, riprap, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Palaniuk: 'Fish the Neko rig in and around brushpiles and flip it to isolated trees where fish are suspended — use 360 imaging to see fish suspended around standing timber and specifically target tree
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on rock, riprap, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Yamamoto: The Chicken Rig is weedless unlike the Neko rig so it can be fished through brush, rocks, and docks. It has big bass appeal with the large Kut Tail profile. It's durable because the worm slides up the line on hookset

KVD: On lowland reservoirs with grass (Guntersville, Toledo Bend, Okeechobee), bass don't leave shallow areas even in extreme cold. They stage in slightly deeper areas adjacent to spawning flats — drains, ditches, depressions, holes in the grass. ...

Wheeler: On highland reservoirs like Cherokee, smallmouth live around holes/depressions (20 ft around the side) — if rock is around them the smallies live there. If a rock vein goes into the middle, they live on those veins. The holes harbor the b...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal winter bass stage at 12-25ft on main lake structure. Falling pressure before a front is often the best winter fishing window. Fish move slightly shallower and feed more aggressively than normal winter behavior.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
